_publications._publicationTitle: Transient inhibition of the ERK pathway prevents cerebellar developmental defects and improves long-term motor functions in murine models of neurofibromatosis type 1.

_contextSnippet: G-responders stayed significantly longer on Rotarod than P-responders, providing a nice correlation between recues in cerebellar developmental defects and motor functions
details: Successfully discriminated between G-responders and P-responders, with G-responders staying significantly longer on Rotarod than P-responders, providing correlation between cerebellar developmental defects and motor functions
